calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-20-2022 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-14-2021 Continued To Oct-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Filippi, via ZOOM, for Robert Thompson, Personal Representative.

The court begins by querying counsel regarding the life estate interest never being transferred. Mr. Fillipi offers oral testimony regarding the matter and states that he may be able to obtain a disclaimer from the Beneficiary. The court then points Mr. Fillipi to timing issues. Mr. Fillipi reports that the parties had purchased a feasible portion of the estate all within nine (9) months. The court advises Mr. Fillipi that the court would prefer that he file a valid assignment or a brief to address the current issues with this matter.

The Accounting petition is continued to Oct-19-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-25-2021 Continued To Sep-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-28-2021 Continued To Aug-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-21-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Filippi appeared by zoom for Robert Thompson.

The Court noted that the will dated August 12, 2011 was previously admitted in The Chancery Court of Harrison County, Mississippi on May 16, 2013. Mr. Filippi stated that he is not aware of any objections. Mr. Filippi also stated that there was a life estate to the Decedent's Sister, that the building burned to the ground, that the Sister no longer lives there and has no interest, that he can't have her disclaim her interest, that the intent is to have the Sister deed her interest back to the estate and asked the Court if it would entertain that. The Court stated that it cannot prejudge a petition. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated August 12, 2011 is admitted to probate. Robert Thompson is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $20,000.00. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for April 20, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Continued To Oct-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
